title: श्रुति गीता
devanagariText:
॥ श्री श्रुति गीता ॥
(श्रीमद्भागवतम् १०.८७ — वेदस्तुतिः)

श्रुतय ऊचुः —

जय जय जह्यजामजित दोषगृभीतगुणां
त्वमसि यदात्मना समवरुद्धसमस्तभगः।
अगजगदोकसामखिलशक्त्यवबोधक ते
क्वचिदजयाऽऽत्मना च चरतोऽनुचरेन्निगमः॥ १॥

उदरमुपासते य ऋषिवर्त्मसु कूर्पदृशः
परिसरपद्धतिं हृदयमारुणयो दहरम्।
तत उदगादनन्ततवतोऽन्तरं मेऽद्य
भवन्नयामहमनन्त महीयसा ते॥ २॥

न घटत उद्भवः प्रकृतिपूरुषयोरजयो-
रुभययुजा भवन्त्यसुभृतो जलबुद्बुदवत्।
त्वयि त इमे ततो विविधनामगुणैः परमे
सरित इवार्णवे मधुनि लिल्युरशेषरसाः॥ ३॥

स्वकृतपुरेष्वमीष्वबहिरन्तरसंवरणं
तव पुरुषं वदन्त्यखिलशक्तिधृतोंऽशकृतम्।
इति नृगतिं विविच्य कवयो निगमावपनं
भवत उपासते ङ्घ्रिमभवं भुवि विश्वसिताः॥ ४॥

न हि परमस्य कश्चिदपरो न परश्च भवे-
द्वियत इवापदस्य तव शून्यतुलां दधतः।
उदरमृते कुतः सविता प्रभवन्ति रुचो
नभसो विना कुतो नभसो न घटेत मृषा॥ ५॥

यदि न समुद्धरन्ति यतयो हृदि कामजटा
दुरधिगमो वसत्कृत एवं प्रभुरस्य चिदात्मा।
क इह नु वेद बतावरजन्म गुणप्रवाहं
तं वयमजं शरणमुपैम भवः परेशम्॥ ६॥

द्रविणदेहमात्मगतिमन्धदृशो विविधो
निगमगिरो हि तन्महदजादयसूचयन्ति।
तदपि न वै भवत्प्रभवमल्पमलर्कपलं
त्वद्गतया भवन्ति भविता च सदा॥ ७॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भागवते दशमस्कन्धे श्रुतिगीता सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shruti Gita ||
(Shrimad Bhagavatam 10.87 — Veda-stutih)

Shrutaya uchuh —

Jaya jaya jahy-ajam-ajita dosha-gribhita-gunam
tvam-asi yad-atmana samavaruddha-samasta-bhagah |
Aga-jagad-okasam-akhila-shakty-avabodhaka te
kvachid-ajaya-atmana cha charato'nucharen-nigamah || 1 ||

Udaram-upasate ya rishi-vartmasu kurpa-drishah
parisara-paddhatim hridayam-arunayo daharam |
Tata udagad-ananta-tavato'ntaram me'dya
bhavan-nayam-aham-ananta mahiyasa te || 2 ||

Na ghatata udbhavah prakriti-purushayoh ajayo-
rubhaya-yuja bhavanty-asubhrito jala-budbuda-vat |
Tvayi ta ime tato vividha-nama-gunaih parame
sarita iva-arnave madhuni lilyur-ashesha-rasah || 3 ||

Svakrita-pureshu amishv-abahir-antara-samvaranam
tava purusham vadanty-akhila-shakti-dhrito'msha-kritam |
Iti nri-gatim vivichya kavayo nigama-avapanam
bhavata upasate'nghrim-abhavam bhuvi vishvasitah || 4 ||

Na hi paramasya kashchid-aparo na parash-cha bhave-
dviyata ivaapadasya tava shunya-tulam dadhatah |
Udaramrite kutah savita prabhavanti rucho
nabhaso vina kuto nabhaso na ghateta mrisha || 5 ||

Yadi na samud-dharanti yatayo hridi kama-jata
duradhigamo vasat-krita evam prabhur-asya chid-atma |
Ka iha nu veda bata-avara-janma guna-pravaham
tam vayam-ajam sharanam-upaima bhavah paresham || 6 ||

Dravina-deham-atma-gatim-andha-drisho vividho
nigama-giro hi tan-mahad-ajadaya-suchayanti |
Tad-api na vai bhavat-prabhavam-alpam-alarka-palam
tvad-gataya bhavanti bhavita cha sada || 7 ||

|| Iti Shrimad Bhagavate Dashamaskandhe Shruti Gita Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
श्रुतियाँ (वेद) बोलीं — जय हो, जय हो! हे अजित! अजा (माया) को जीतिए जो दोषों से गुणों को ग्रहण करती है। आप स्वयं समस्त ऐश्वर्यों से सम्पन्न हैं। चर-अचर जगत् के समस्त प्राणियों की शक्ति को प्रबुद्ध करने वाले आप हैं — वेद आपका ही अनुसरण करते हैं॥ १॥

जो ऋषियों के मार्ग पर चलते हैं वे उदर (ब्रह्मलोक) की उपासना करते हैं, कुछ हृदयाकाश (दहर) की — हे अनन्त! आपकी महिमा से मैं आज आपके अन्तर को जानना चाहता हूँ॥ २॥

प्रकृति और पुरुष — दोनों अजन्मा हैं — इनसे उत्पत्ति सम्भव नहीं। इन दोनों के संयोग से प्राणी जल-बुद्बुद की भाँति उत्पन्न होते हैं। विविध नाम-गुणों वाले ये सब आप परम में ऐसे लीन होते हैं जैसे नदियाँ समुद्र में, जैसे रस मधु में॥ ३॥

आपके बनाए शरीर-नगरों में बाहर-भीतर व्याप्त आपके पुरुष (जीवात्मा) को सर्वशक्तिधर का अंश कहते हैं। कवि (ज्ञानी) मनुष्य-गति को विवेचन कर आपके चरणों की उपासना करते हैं॥ ४॥

परम का न कोई अपर है न कोई पर — जैसे आकाश में कोई विशेष पद नहीं। आपका शून्य-तुल्य स्वरूप है — बिना आपके प्रकाश कहाँ से, बिना आकाश के आकाश कैसे?॥ ५॥

यदि यति लोग हृदय की काम-जटाओं को नहीं उखाड़ते तो चिदात्मा प्रभु दुर्गम ही रहते हैं। कौन इस अनन्त गुण-प्रवाह को जानता है? हम उन अज, परेश की शरण लेते हैं॥ ६॥
benefits: Reveals the Vedic understanding of the Supreme Being
Invokes deep philosophical wisdom about brahman
Traditionally recited for removal of spiritual ignorance at its root
Elevates consciousness to the highest plane
Purifies the intellect for Vedantic realization
Invokes the blessing of the fruit of studying all four vedas
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Shruti Gita (श्रुति गीता) stands as a sacred prayer. Attributed to Shrimad Bhagavata Purana 10.87, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to purify the intellect for Vedantic realization and remove spiritual ignorance at its root. It is also said to reveal the Vedic understanding of the Supreme Being. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ekadashi and Guru Purnima. At 7 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Shruti Gita in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shruti Gita from Bhagavata Purana 10.87 in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. The Vedas' prayer to the Supreme Lord.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shruti Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shruti Gita (श्रुति गीता — 'Song of the Vedas') is a profound philosophical hymn from the 87th chapter of the 10th canto of the Shrimad Bhagavata Purana (Bhagavatam 10.87 — Veda-stuti). The hymn is uttered by the personified Vedas (Shrutis) themselves in the supreme cosmological setting: at the dawn of creation, while Bhagavan Sri Krishna (the Supreme Brahman) lies in yogic slumber on the cosmic ocean before manifesting the universe, the four Vedas in their personified form (Shrutaya — feminine plural) approach Him and sing this magnificent hymn praising the supreme Brahman / Narayana / Krishna. The hymn is regarded as the supreme Vedanta-stuti of the entire Bhagavata Purana, condensing the highest non-dual / qualified non-dual / dvaita teachings of the Upanishads about the nature of the Supreme Brahman. It is recited on Ekadashi and during Bhagavata-saptaha pravachanas. (Source: Shrimad Bhagavata Purana, Skandha 10, Adhyaya 87.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the cosmological setting of the Shruti Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shruti Gita is narrated within the framing dialogue of the entire Bhagavata Purana — Suta Goswami narrates it to the assembled rishis at Naimisharanya, recounting how Maharaja Parikshit, in the seven days of his life remaining before the curse of Takshaka would take effect, requested Shukadeva Goswami to teach him the supreme means of moksha. Within this framing, Shukadeva tells how the great sage Narayana Rishi (in his meditation at Badarikashrama) once recited this Veda-stuti to sage Narada, having himself heard it from Sanandana, one of the four Kumaras, who had himself heard it as the Vedas' own song to Krishna at the dawn of creation. Thus the hymn carries unparalleled parampara-authority — it is the song of the Vedas themselves to the Supreme Lord, transmitted through the four Kumaras to Narayana Rishi to Narada to Vyasa to Shuka to Parikshit to Suta to the rishis of Naimisharanya. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 10.87.1-9.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the opening verse Jaya jaya jahyajam ajita?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse of the Shruti Gita is one of the most profound in the entire Bhagavata: Jaya jaya jahyajam ajita dosha-gribhita-gunam, tvam-asi yad-atmana samavaruddha-samasta-bhagah; aga-jagad-okasam-akhila-shakty-avabodhaka te, kvachid-ajaya-atmana cha charato-anucharen-nigamah — 'Glory, glory! O Unconquered One (Ajita), conquer (jahy — destroy) the unborn maya (aja) which has grasped the gunas through the dosha (defects of the jiva). For You alone are the Self in whom all aishvarya (powers and glories) is fully contained. You are the Awakener of all powers in moving and unmoving beings; the Vedas (nigama) follow You alone, even when You sport with the unborn maya in some places.' The verse establishes the supreme position of Krishna as the Para Brahman beyond maya, and the Vedas as His own self-revelation. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 10.87.14.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Shruti Gita called the supreme Vedanta of the Bhagavata?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shruti Gita is regarded by all Vaishnava acharyas as the supreme Vedantic chapter of the entire Bhagavata Purana because it directly addresses the foundational questions of Vedanta in profoundly compact verses: (1) the nature of Brahman as both transcendent (nirguna) and the indweller of all hearts (saguna) — verse 1, (2) the tension between the inner-heart contemplation (dahara-vidya of Aruni-Upakosala) and the cosmic worship (verse 2), (3) the unborn nature of prakriti and purusha and the rise of jivas like bubbles in water from their union (verse 3), (4) the worshipper's surrender to the Lord's lotus feet alone as the supreme refuge (verse 4), (5) the supreme paradox of Brahman being neither one nor many, neither finite nor infinite, of the nature of shunya-tula (verse 5), (6) the need to extract the kama-jata (root of desires) from the heart in order to realise the chid-atma Lord (verse 6), and (7) the inadequacy of mere wealth-and-body identification of those of blind vision (verse 7). It is the supreme philosophical hymn of the Bhagavata. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 10.87; classical Vaishnava commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are the Vedas described as personified feminine in the Shruti Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vedas are addressed throughout the Shruti Gita as Shrutayah (श्रुतयः — feminine plural of Shruti, 'that which is heard') because in classical Hindu cosmology, the Vedas are conceived as eternal living entities who 'breathe forth' from the Supreme Lord at every cosmic dawn (kalpa-arambha) — they are not human compositions but apaurusheya (impersonal, eternal) divine sound-revelation. The four Vedas (Rig, Yajur, Sama, Atharva) personified as Shrutayah / Shruti-devis approach the Supreme Lord — much as Sri Lakshmi or Bhumi Devi personify aspects of the divine feminine — and offer their own song of praise. This is a magnificent literary device that expresses the foundational Hindu theological position: the Vedas themselves bow down to and worship the Supreme Lord (Krishna / Narayana) who is greater than the Vedas. The Vedas are not the highest reality — the Para Brahman whom the Vedas describe is. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 10.87 introduction; classical Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Shruti Gita be recited and what are its benefits?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shruti Gita is most powerfully recited on Ekadashi (the bi-monthly Vishnu day, especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi in Margashirsha), during the Bhagavata-saptaha (the seven-day pravachana of the Bhagavata Purana), Krishna Janmashtami, Guru Purnima (Ashadha Shukla Purnima — when devotees honour Vyasa, the compiler of the Bhagavata), every Thursday (Guruvar), and during Chaturmasya. Tradition assigns the following blessings to sincere recitation: (1) the supreme phala promised in the closing verses — direct realisation of the Para Brahman as the Atman, (2) destruction of avidya (spiritual ignorance) at its root through the Vedantic insights of the hymn, (3) the merit of having heard the Vedas themselves singing to Krishna — the supreme Vaishnava darshana, (4) qualification for the highest Vedantic understanding combining Advaita, Vishishtadvaita and Dvaita perspectives, (5) the same merit as studying the four Vedas in their entirety (since the Vedas themselves bow to Krishna in this hymn), (6) preparation of the mind for moksha through Krishna-prema, and (7) admission into the supreme parampara of Bhagavata study from Krishna to Sanandana to Narayana Rishi to Narada to Vyasa to Shukadeva. (Source: Shruti Gita phala; classical Bhagavata commentaries.)
